value: MCP can edit Puck Fields panel inputs! Steps: 1) Click a draggable section in preview iframe (uid from snapshot). 2) Fields panel opens on right with textbox elements. 3) Use mcp__chrome-devtools__fill(uid, value) to set field value. NOTE: fill() APPENDS to React inputs instead of replacing. To replace: click field → press_key(Meta+A) → type_text(new_value). But Meta+A doesn't work cross-iframe. Best approach: use fill() for adding test markers, then revert via DB SQL.

After fill() on a Puck field: status changes from '✓ Guardado' to '● Sin guardar'. 'Guardar borrador' button enables. Clicking save shows 'Guardando...' then '✓ Guardado'. DB confirms change persisted. Full edit→save→verify cycle works via MCP.

When Flutter and Next.js run on different ports (e.g., :8888 vs :3000), evaluate_script cannot access iframe.contentDocument (cross-origin). Use take_snapshot instead — MCP accessibility tree reads cross-iframe. fill(), click(), type_text() work on nested iframe UIDs via snapshot.
Phase 2 page selector visible in Puck header as 'Inicio ▼' button. Dropdown lists all pages. Clicking a page reloads editor with ?page=xxx. Unsaved changes dialog shown if dirty.

All 10 steps of updated puck_editor template pass: puck_loads, inspector_hidden, header_actions, iframe_edit (via fill), save_flow, save_persisted (DB verified), preview_toggle (header+footer), tab_keepalive (1 round-trip), page_selector (9 pages in dropdown), no_console_errors. Score: 10/10.
Page selector dropdown shows: Inicio (homepage), Destinos/Hoteles/Actividades/Traslados/Paquetes (category), Nosotros/Contacto (static), test (custom). 9 pages total for demo-travel website.
When editor has dirty state and page reloads, browser shows beforeunload dialog. MCP navigate_page(reload) auto-accepts it. This confirms the dirty state guard works at browser level too.
Console shows 2x SVG path errors: '<path> attribute d: Expected moveto...undefined'. These are Puck icon rendering issues — classify as asset_noise, not runtime errors.
